interpretation: An independent structure predictor (Boltz-2) reproduces the conformal audit verdict: certified edges get high Boltz-2 ipTM (mean 0.71), dropped edges get low (mean 0.46), certified>dropped p=0.021; AF-M and Boltz-2 rankings agree strongly (Spearman 0.80, p=0.002). Cross-architecture divergence therefore carries a correctness signal and can be integrated as a nonconformity feature. Underpowered pilot (n=12, no score-matched controls possible because certification and AF-M score are near-separable on this map); a funded scale-up (Anthropic AI-for-Science, ~$2k Modal) is the path to a calibrated feature.
caveats: Small n; certified pairs also have higher AF-M score, so this pilot shows Boltz-2 AGREES with AF-M/conformal, not yet that disagreement adds INDEPENDENT signal beyond score. The score-matched test needs a map where certification and raw score are not separable.
